channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-07-22
item.description:
It's been an exciting week in our open-source research community, with notable progress across multiple projects. The `scienceverse/metacheck` repository saw significant updates to its `data_check` branch, including the addition of a "reproducibility_check" and integration with JASP and Jamovi functionality, marking a major step forward in data validation. Meanwhile, the `marton-balazs-kovacs/tenzing` project received improvements to its master branch, with reworded text and italicized testimonials for enhanced emphasis. These updates demonstrate the community's commitment to enhancing research tools and methodologies. The simultaneous updates to `marton-balazs-kovacs/tenzing`'s master and gh-pages branches on July 18, 2026, also highlight the importance of collaboration and version control in our projects. As our community continues to grow and evolve, we celebrate these achievements and look forward to the innovative developments that will emerge from these connections.

21 commits across 4 active repositories this week.

New releases: oddpub v7.3.0

Most active:

tenzing7 commits
oddpub7 commits
metacheck6 commits
escicheck1 commits

item.title: metacheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The `data_check` branch was the focus of recent work, with the addition of a "reproducibility_check" as stated in the commit message "add reproducibility_check". This change, made on July 22, 2026, specifically added this new check to the repository. On July 18, 2026, another commit to the `data_check` branch added "japs jamovi", as per the commit message "add japs jamovi", which suggests the integration of JASP and Jamovi functionality. The `data_check` branch was the sole branch worked on during this period. No other branches were modified in these commits.

1 commit(s) today:

  • db4cf0f [data_check] add reproducibility_check — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The `main` branch was updated with a sync from `escicheckapp@7c4eb22`, which corresponds to `effectcheck v0.6.14`. The `main` branch was also previously updated with a sync from `escicheckapp@ec9eb9f`, which corresponds to `effectcheck v0.6.13`. These commits suggest that the repository is being kept in sync with another project, `escicheckapp`, with the latest versions of `effectcheck` being reflected in the `main` branch. The specific changes brought in by these syncs are not detailed in the commit messages, but they are associated with issues `#27` and `#26`, respectively. No other branches were worked on in these commits, with all changes being applied to the `main` branch.

1 commit(s) today:

  • e7b7351 [main] sync from escicheckapp@7c4eb22 (effectcheck v0.6.14) (#27) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →
